source_pr.title: variable:  fix sysvar datarace with deep copy
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/24712
Fixes https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/24735

Problem Summary:

The SysVar struct is not entirely immutable, since SEM (in tests) and server startup may modify the .Value for certain sysvars. This causes a data race because callers of `GetSysVar`/`GetSysVars` previously did not hold deep copied of the sysvars.

Deep copy is the simplest fix for this problem.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

The functions `GetSysVar`/`GetSysVars` now return copies of sysvars to avoid data races.

### Related changes

- PR to update `pingcap/docs`/`pingcap/docs-cn`:
- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

Side effects

- Performance regression
    - Consumes more MEM

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- A potential data race when reading system variable values has been fixed. This data race is only known to affect the testsuite and is not known to impact any applications.
